name: DiskRaptor v0.2.5
body:
## DiskRaptor v0.2.5

| Platform | Downloads | Notes |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| **Windows** | `.msi` installer or `.exe` | Windows 10+ |
| **macOS** | `.dmg` or `DiskRaptor-macOS-x64` | macOS 11+ (unsigned) |
| **Linux** 🐧 | `.AppImage` | ✅ Works on ALL Ubuntu/Debian versions |
| **Linux (20.04/22.04)** 🐧 | `.deb` | ✅ Works on Ubuntu 20.04, 22.04 |
| **Linux (24.04+)** 🐧 | `.deb` | ❌ NOT supported — use **AppImage** instead |

## 🧪 UI Test Results

See [TEST_MATRIX.md](TEST_MATRIX.md) for the full cross-platform test matrix.

> ⚠️ macOS & Linux builds are unsigned. On macOS: right-click → Open.
>
> **Linux**:
> - **AppImage** (🟢 recommended for ALL versions):
>   - If you get `libfuse.so.2` error, use:
>     ```
>     APPIMAGE_EXTRACT_AND_RUN=1 ./disk-raptor_*.AppImage
>     ```
>   - Or install FUSE 2: `sudo apt install libfuse2`
>   - **Debian**: If WebView doesn't appear, install:
>     ```
>     sudo apt install libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 libgtk-3-0 librsvg2-2
>     APPIMAGE_EXTRACT_AND_RUN=1 GTK_THEME=Adwaita ./disk-raptor_*.AppImage
>     ```
> - **.deb** (🟢 Ubuntu 20.04 / 22.04): `sudo apt install ./disk-raptor_*.deb`
> - **.deb** (🔴 Ubuntu 24.04+): NOT supported — webkit2gtk-4.0 was removed in 24.04. Use **AppImage** instead.
>
> **Windows**: Run the `.msi` installer or the `.exe` directly.

name: DiskRaptor v0.2.4
body:
## DiskRaptor v0.2.4

| Platform | Downloads |
|----------|-----------|
| **Windows** | `.msi` installer or `DiskRaptor-Windows-x64.exe` |
| **macOS** | `.dmg` or `DiskRaptor-macOS-x64` binary |
| **Linux** | `.AppImage` or `DiskRaptor-Linux-x64` binary |

## 🧪 UI Test Results

See [TEST_MATRIX.md](TEST_MATRIX.md) for the full cross-platform test matrix.

> ⚠️ macOS & Linux builds are unsigned. On macOS: right-click → Open.
>
> **Linux**:
> - **AppImage** (recommended): `chmod +x disk-raptor_*.AppImage && ./disk-raptor_*.AppImage`
> - **.deb** (if AppImage doesn't work): `sudo apt install ./disk-raptor_*.deb`
>
> **Windows**: Run the `.msi` installer or the `.exe` directly.
